description: Display computed property values or formatted text at a grid position. Use ONLY when the user explicitly requests displaying values or text. Content is in MATH MODE by default — all text is rendered as math. Plain text must be wrapped in text() to display as normal text. Example: "text(Step 1:) x^2 + y^2 = r^2" renders 'Step 1:' as plain text and the equation as math.
utility: Display computed property values or formatted text at grid position

arguments.name: content
arguments.description: Content string in math mode by default. Wrap plain text in text() — e.g., "text(Result:) x = 5"

alternates: desc(at(r, c), "x^2 + y^2 = 1") - math expression (default mode)
desc(at(r, c), "text(Step 1:) x = 5") - mixed plain text and math
desc(at(r, c), "text(This is plain text)") - plain text only
desc(at(r, c), "text", c(red), fs(14)) - styled text

examples.expression: desc_3 = desc(at(26,32), "d_1 + d_2 = 2a = 2 cdot :{p(ellipse_1, semimajor)}:.2f = :{2 * p(ellipse_1, semimajor)}:.2f")

examples.scenario: Mixed plain text and math formula
Step instruction text (plain text wrapped in text())
